forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9e80'
+project: local-review
+title: Inline full changed-file source in review prompt (eliminate redundant re-reads)
+---
+## What
+
+The review fan-out prompt hands each validator a **bounded** source slice per changed file (`render_file_block` → `bounded_slice`), but the local qwen model re-reads every changed file anyway via `read_file`/`glob`/`grep` before reasoning. Calcutron eval logs show the changed file's content is prefilled **twice** (once as the inlined slice, once as the `read_file` tool result the model fetches regardless), and ~82% of review wall-clock is spent in tool-call round-trips, not decoding (~78 tok/s decode is not the bottleneck).
+
+Root cause: the bounded slice is incomplete, so the model reads the whole file to be safe. Fix by **giving in and inlining the whole changed file**, clearly framed so the model knows it does NOT need to read it. Keep `read_file`/`grep`/`glob` advertised — they remain essential for cross-file work (duplication evidence cites *other* files, refactor suggestions need them). Only the **changed** files get pre-provided in full so there's no reason to re-read them.
+
+The full file text is already available at the slice-build site — `scope.rs:244` passes `resolved.after_content.get(file)` into `bounded_slice`, which currently discards most of it.
+
+Files to modify:
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — populate `FileWork.source_slice` (or a new `full_source` field) with the **complete** `after_content` for the file, gated by a size cap. For files whose full content exceeds the cap (token budget is `max_tokens: 16384` per turn), fall back to the existing `bounded_slice` plus an explicit note that the file was too large to inline in full and `read_file` should be used for the remainder. Keep `bounded_slice` for that fallback path only.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs` — in `render_file_block`, reframe the per-file block so the intent is explicit: a clearly-fenced full-file section labeled as the complete current contents ("here is the full file — you do NOT need to read it"), followed by the "what changed" semantic diff. Update `OUTPUT_CONTRACT` / the file-block preamble so reads are scoped to *other* files (cross-file duplication, callers, type defs), not the changed files already provided in full.
+
+Constraints:
+- Do NOT remove or hide the intrinsic file tools — cross-file duplication detection and refactor suggestions depend on them.
+- Keep the validator prefix (cached/primed part) unchanged; the full-file payload lives in the per-fork file payload, not the shared prefix.
+- Respect a token/byte cap so a large changed file cannot blow the prompt budget.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] For a changed file under the size cap, the rendered payload contains the file's **complete** source in a clearly-labeled fenced block, plus the semantic diff, plus explicit text that the file need not be read.
+- [ ] For a changed file over the size cap, the payload falls back to the bounded slice with a note directing the model to `read_file` for the rest.
+- [ ] `read_file`/`glob`/`grep` remain advertised to the review session (no tool gating).
+- [ ] The cached validator prefix bytes are unchanged (full-file content stays in the per-file/per-fork payload).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs` unit test: `render_file_payload` for a small file emits the full source (assert a line that `bounded_slice` would have trimmed is present) and the "you do not need to read this file" framing.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` unit test: a file whose full content exceeds the cap yields the bounded-slice fallback + the read-for-the-rest note; a file under the cap yields the full content.
+- [ ] Unit test asserting the rendered prefix (shared/cached sections) does not contain the file source, so prefix caching is unaffected.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::` → green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing render/scope tests first, then implement.
\ No newline at end of file

-- Do NOT remove or hide the intrinsic file tools — cross-file duplication detection and refactor suggestions depend on them.
-- Keep the validator prefix (cached/primed part) unchanged; the full-file payload lives in the per-fork file payload, not the shared prefix.

-- [ ] `read_file`/`glob`/`grep` remain advertised to the review session (no tool gating).

\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -15,23 +15,23 @@
 The full file text is already available at the slice-build site — `scope.rs:244` passes `resolved.after_content.get(file)` into `bounded_slice`, which currently discards most of it.

 Files to modify:
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — populate `FileWork.source_slice` (or a new `full_source` field) with the **complete** `after_content` for the file, gated by a size cap. For files whose full content exceeds the cap (token budget is `max_tokens: 16384` per turn), fall back to the existing `bounded_slice` plus an explicit note that the file was too large to inline in full and `read_file` should be used for the remainder. Keep `bounded_slice` for that fallback path only.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` — populate `FileWork.source_slice` (or a new `full_source` field) with the **complete** `after_content` for the file. The constraint is the model's **context window**, NOT the 16384 generation cap (`max_tokens` in the prompt `_meta` bounds the *reply*, not the input). Typical source files inline whole with ample headroom (primed prefixes are only ~5k tokens). A guard is only needed for a pathologically large file relative to the configured context window: in that rare case fall back to the existing `bounded_slice` plus an explicit note that the file was too large to inline in full and `read_file` should be used for the remainder. Pick the cap from the model/context-window config, not a hardcoded 16k. Keep `bounded_slice` for that fallback path only.
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs` — in `render_file_block`, reframe the per-file block so the intent is explicit: a clearly-fenced full-file section labeled as the complete current contents ("here is the full file — you do NOT need to read it"), followed by the "what changed" semantic diff. Update `OUTPUT_CONTRACT` / the file-block preamble so reads are scoped to *other* files (cross-file duplication, callers, type defs), not the changed files already provided in full.

 Constraints:
 - Do NOT remove or hide the intrinsic file tools — cross-file duplication detection and refactor suggestions depend on them.
 - Keep the validator prefix (cached/primed part) unchanged; the full-file payload lives in the per-fork file payload, not the shared prefix.
-- Respect a token/byte cap so a large changed file cannot blow the prompt budget.
+- The size guard (if any) keys off the configured context window, not the 16384 generation cap.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] For a changed file under the size cap, the rendered payload contains the file's **complete** source in a clearly-labeled fenced block, plus the semantic diff, plus explicit text that the file need not be read.
-- [ ] For a changed file over the size cap, the payload falls back to the bounded slice with a note directing the model to `read_file` for the rest.
+- [ ] For a changed file that fits the context window, the rendered payload contains the file's **complete** source in a clearly-labeled fenced block, plus the semantic diff, plus explicit text that the file need not be read.
+- [ ] For a changed file too large for the context-window budget, the payload falls back to the bounded slice with a note directing the model to `read_file` for the rest.
 - [ ] `read_file`/`glob`/`grep` remain advertised to the review session (no tool gating).
 - [ ] The cached validator prefix bytes are unchanged (full-file content stays in the per-file/per-fork payload).

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs` unit test: `render_file_payload` for a small file emits the full source (assert a line that `bounded_slice` would have trimmed is present) and the "you do not need to read this file" framing.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` unit test: a file whose full content exceeds the cap yields the bounded-slice fallback + the read-for-the-rest note; a file under the cap yields the full content.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs` unit test: a file whose full content exceeds the context-window cap yields the bounded-slice fallback + the read-for-the-rest note; a file under the cap yields the full content.
 - [ ] Unit test asserting the rendered prefix (shared/cached sections) does not contain the file source, so prefix caching is unaffected.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators review::` → green.

\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing render/scope tests first, then implement.
+
+## Implementation notes
+- `scope.rs`: added `MAX_INLINE_SOURCE_BYTES` (32 KiB, documented as keyed off the model's **context window**, explicitly NOT the `DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS`/16384 generation cap) and a new `inline_or_slice(after, entities) -> (String, bool)` helper. A file under the cap returns its complete `after_content` (`inlined_full = true`); an oversized file returns `bounded_slice` + `OVERSIZED_FILE_READ_NOTE` (`inlined_full = false`). `FileWork` gained an `inlined_full: bool` field; `bounded_slice` is retained for the fallback path only.
+- `fleet.rs`: `render_file_block` now frames a full-inline file under `### Full current contents` with explicit "You do not need to read this file" text, and an oversized file under `### Source slice (partial …)` directing the model to `read_file`. `OUTPUT_CONTRACT` gained a `## Reading files` section that scopes intrinsic reads to OTHER files while keeping `read_file`/`glob`/`grep` advertised. The shared/cached prefix carries no file source (verified by test).
+- Full-file content lives only in the per-fork payload (`render_file_payload`), so the primed/cached prefix bytes are unchanged — confirmed by `prefix_and_payload_split_is_byte_stable_and_composes_the_monolithic_prompt`.
+- Verification: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators --lib` → 266 passed / 0 failed; `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-validators --all-targets` → clean; downstream `swissarmyhammer-tools` builds clean.
\ No newline at end of file

The shared/cached prefix carries no file source (verified by test).
 - Full-file content lives only in the per-fork payload (`render_file_payload`), so the primed/cached prefix bytes are unchanged — confirmed by `prefix_and_payload_split_is_byte_stable_and_composes_the_monolithic_prompt`.
-- Verification: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators --lib` → 266 passed / 0 failed; `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-validators --all-targets` → clean; downstream `swissarmyhammer-tools` builds clean.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Verification: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators --lib` → 266 passed / 0 failed; `cargo clippy -p swissarmyhammer-validators --all-targets` → clean; downstream `swissarmyhammer-tools` builds clean.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-14 14:47)
+
+> ⚠️ 5/45 review tasks failed — results are INCOMPLETE.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/drive.rs:1` — Path traversal: `read_text_file_under_repo` builds the read path from the agent's `fs/read_text_file` request with no confinement to `repo_root` — `if req.path.is_absolute() { req.path.clone() } else { repo_root.join(&req.path) }` reads any absolute path verbatim, and a relative path containing `..` segments escapes `repo_root` because the join is never canonicalized and checked with `starts_with(repo_root)`. The function's own doc comment asserts the read is 'resolved under repo_root', which the code does not enforce, so a confused or compromised review agent can exfiltrate arbitrary files (e.g. `../../../../etc/passwd`) through the auto-answering client. Severity is warning rather than blocker because the requester is the locally-run review LLM already executing with the user's privileges, bounding real-world impact. Confine the read to `repo_root`: reject absolute paths (or treat them as relative), then `repo_root.join(&req.path)`, `std::fs::canonicalize(&path)`, and verify the canonical result `starts_with(canonical_repo_root)` before `read_to_string`, returning an `invalid_params` error otherwise. This keeps the legitimate in-repo reads working while closing the `..`/absolute escape.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:230` — `run_validator_fleet` is ~61 lines of actual code (body spans fleet.rs:237-308) and braids three distinct phases — building the `pending` batch tasks (incl. an inline `Pending` struct and the fork-vs-monolithic submit match), collecting/tallying every task result, and releasing the prefix pin. The mixed responsibilities make it hard to read and impossible to unit-test the submit and collect halves independently. Extract the submission loop (the `for batch in validator.files.chunks(...)` block producing `Vec<Pending>`) into `submit_validator_batches(...)` and the collection loop (`for task in pending` accumulating findings/failed) into `collect_validator_batches(...)`, leaving `run_validator_fleet` as prime → submit → collect → unpin.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:1` — `bounded_slice` nests four levels deep — `for entity in entities` → `if let Some(body)` → `if let Some(content) = after` → `if let Some(window) = hunk_window(...)` — which crosses the 4+ nesting flag threshold and makes the per-entity windowing logic hard to follow. Flatten with early-`continue`/`let ... else` and a helper: e.g. `let Some(body) = &entity.after_content else { continue };` then `if let (Some(content), Some(window)) = (after, after.and_then(|c| hunk_window(c, body)))` — or extract an `entity_sections(entity, after)` helper returning the body+window pair.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:214` — `scope_review` is ~95 lines of actual code (body spans lines 221–325), well over the ~50-line limit — it performs six distinct stages (resolve, semantic-diff, group entities, match validators per file, run probe cache, assemble work-list) in one body, making it hard to read and test each stage in isolation. Extract the cohesive stages into helpers, e.g. `group_entities_by_file`, `match_validators(loader, &resolved.files)`, `compute_file_facts`, and `assemble_validator_work`, leaving `scope_review` as a short orchestration that calls them in sequence.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:299` — Confirmed findings are written with `refuted_by: Some(RefutingLayer::Agent)`, but the field name says 'refuted' — so a finding that was *confirmed* (not refuted) serializes as `{"confirmed": true, ..., "refuted_by": "Agent"}`. The name contradicts the value, and since `VerifiedFinding` derives `Serialize`/`Deserialize` (types.rs:116) this misleading name is part of the wire contract that downstream consumers see. `refuted_by.is_some()` cannot be used to mean 'was refuted'. Rename the field to its documented meaning — `decided_by` / `adjudicated_by` (a `From` rename, since it's serialized) — and fix the verify.rs module doc (lines 28-29) to say 'which layer reached the verdict' to match types.rs:127. Alternatively, if the name is intended literally, set `refuted_by: None` whenever `confirmed == true` so `Some(_)` genuinely means refuted.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:432` — `render_probe_evidence` re-implements the fan-out renderer of the same name in `fleet.rs:831` instead of sharing it. The inner row-rendering loop (file_path, `:line`, `` `symbol` ``, ` @ {sim:.2}`, ` — {detail}`) and the `_No probe evidence._` / `(no rows)` sentinels are byte-for-byte identical; the only divergence is the `({kind})` annotation on the per-probe header. Two copies of the evidence format will drift apart and must be fixed twice. Extract one shared `render_probe_evidence(out: &mut String, results: &[ProbeResult], show_kind: bool)` (or a `with_kind` variant) into a module both stages import; have `fleet::render_file_block` pass `&file.probe_results` and `verify::render_verify_prompt` pass `&candidate.probe_results`, with the `({kind})` header as the single parameterized difference.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:30` — Public struct `FleetConfig` has a documented field but no doc comment on the type itself, so `cargo doc`/`#[deny(missing_docs)]` sees an undocumented public API and readers get no statement of what the config governs. Add a `///` line above `pub struct FleetConfig` describing it as the fan-out fleet's batching policy (the knob that bounds task count on a large diff).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/fleet.rs:47` — Public struct `FleetOutcome` documents its fields but not the type, leaving the returned-aggregate contract (findings + attempted/failed tally) undocumented at the type level where consumers look first. Add a `///` summarizing it as the merged result of a fan-out run, and why the failure tally exists (to distinguish a saturated run from a clean diff).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/probes.rs:37` — Public enum `ProbeOp` documents each variant but has no doc comment on the enum itself, so the public type carries no top-level explanation of what it represents (the code_context op a probe runs). Add a `///` above `pub enum ProbeOp` naming it as the code_context operation the runner interprets per catalog entry.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/probes.rs:63` — Public static `CATALOG` — the single source-of-truth probe table the module docs reference — has no doc comment, so the exported constant lacks the explanation the mandate requires for public constants. Add a `///` stating it is the authoritative table of the three known probes (`callers`, `duplicates`, `similar`).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/probes.rs:86` — Public struct `ChangeEntry` documents its fields but not the type, so the diff-derived entity type that probes bind to is undocumented at the type level. Add a `///` describing it as one changed entity from the semantic diff that probes bind their arguments from.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/probes.rs:105` — Public struct `FileChange` has no type-level doc comment (only its `entities` field is documented), leaving the probe runner's primary input type undocumented. Add a `///` naming it as the set of changed entities a probe run is bound against.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/probes.rs:118` — Public struct `ProbeRow` documents its fields but not the type, so the evidence-row type injected into review prompts is undocumented at the type level. Add a `///` describing it as one evidence row a probe produced (a duplicate location, a caller site, a similar chunk).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/probes.rs:140` — Public struct `ProbeResult` has documented fields but no doc comment on the type, leaving the per-probe result type undocumented. Add a `///` describing it as one probe's result bound to a single diff-derived target.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:60` — The validator-name literal "scope" is hand-written at three separate `AvpError::Validator { validator: "scope".to_string(), .. }` sites; a typo or rename in one place silently diverges the error's `validator` field from the others, and the value is the module's identity rather than a one-off. Hoist `const SCOPE_VALIDATOR: &str = "scope";` (alongside the existing `HUNK_WINDOW_LINES`/`HEADER_LINES` consts) and reference it at all three sites so the validator name changes in one place.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:300` — `read_at_ref` and `read_working` collapse every failure to `None` via `.ok()` / `String::from_utf8(...).ok()`, conflating "path absent at this ref" (the intended Added/Deleted signal) with a real failure (permission error, or a non-UTF8/binary blob). A binary or unreadable tracked file then silently diffs as wholly added or removed rather than being recognized as unreadable. Only map the genuine not-found case to `None` (match libgit2's not-found / `io::ErrorKind::NotFound`) and propagate other errors as `AvpError::Context(..)`, so a binary or unreadable file is not misclassified as an add/delete.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs:40` — `FleetTally::new(attempted: usize, failed: usize)` takes two adjacent same-type `usize` parameters with distinct meanings, so `FleetTally::new(failed, attempted)` transposes silently with no compile error — and since both fields are already `pub`, the positional constructor adds a footgun without adding ergonomics over a self-documenting struct literal. Drop `new` in favor of the named-field struct literal `FleetTally { attempted, failed }` (already used by the `From<&FleetOutcome>` impl), or wrap the counts in distinct newtypes if a constructor is wanted.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs:118` — `synthesize` is ~55 lines of actual code (body 119–190), marginally over the ~50 limit — the per-severity section-rendering loop (152–177) is a self-contained unit that could stand alone. Extract the section loop into `render_sections(&kept, &mut counts, &mut markdown)` (or a function returning `(String, per-severity counts)`), shrinking `synthesize` to header + flags + a single call.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/types.rs:1` — `extract_json_value` strings together four distinct extraction strategies, each with its own 2–3-level `if let`/boolean-guard nest (`if let Some(start)` → `if let Some(end)` → `if content.starts_with(open) && content.ends_with(close)`), so a reader must hold four parallel fallback shapes in mind at once. Extract each strategy into a small helper returning `Option<&str>` (e.g. `from_json_fence`, `from_bare_fence`, `from_balanced`, `from_outer`) and make the body `from_json_fence(t).or_else(|| from_bare_fence(t)).or_else(...).unwrap_or(t)` — same logic, one level deep.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:0` — Public struct `VerifyOutcome` has no item-level doc comment. Its `verified` field and its methods (`confirmed_count`, `refuted_count`, `confirmed`) are documented, but the type — the public return of `verify_findings` — has no summary describing that it carries every candidate's verdict. Add a summary line, e.g. `/// The outcome of the verify stage: every candidate's verdict, each tagged with the `RefutingLayer` that decided it.`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/verify.rs:21` — Public struct `Candidate` has no item-level doc comment. Its fields are each documented, but the type itself lacks the one-line summary that tells a caller what a `Candidate` represents (a fan-out finding plus its reused source slice and probe results awaiting verification). Add a summary line above the struct, e.g. `/// A fan-out candidate finding plus the ground-truth context (source slice + reused probe results) the verify stage refutes it against.`.
\ No newline at end of file

reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,8 +1,8 @@
 ---
 assignees:
 - claude-code
-position_column: done
-position_ordinal: ffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffa980
+position_column: review
+position_ordinal: '80'
 project: local-review
 title: Inline full changed-file source in review prompt (eliminate redundant re-reads)
 ---
